short_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, Caught by Buttler!! 118kph, slower ball on a length, Starc stays leg-side to slash it away and get
full_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, Caught by Buttler!! 118kph, slower ball on a length, Starc stays leg-side to slash it away and gets a thin edge. Buttler leans forward and takes a low catch.

short_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, , short, ball, in-angler, leg-lined, Starc swivels and pulls over backward square leg. Swung hard
full_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, , short, ball, in-angler, leg-lined, Starc swivels and pulls over backward square leg. Swung hard and dispatched it over the ropes

short_text: Tymal Mills to Zampa, Zampa Run Out!! 115kph, slower ball outside off, Zampa moves across a long way to scoop and misses
full_text: Tymal Mills to Zampa, Zampa Run Out!! 115kph, slower ball outside off, Zampa moves across a long way to scoop and misses. Buttler slightly fumbles and the batters try to nab a single on byes. The keeper fires a throw, Mills collects it and breaks the stumps at the bowler's end to catch Zampa short of his crease.

short_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, leg byes, 1 run, , Starc is cramped for room by this in-slanting short of length ball. Looks to pl
full_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, leg byes, 1 run, , Starc is cramped for room by this in-slanting short of length ball. Looks to play across the line and is beaten on the inside edge. The ball thuds into his thigh and dies down on the off-side as the batters steal a run

short_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, 2 runs, , shortened length, pulled to long-on's left and the batters come back for the second. The
full_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, 2 runs, , shortened length, pulled to long-on's left and the batters come back for the second. The throw comes to the bowler's end and Zampa is comfortably in

short_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, , Mills has been the most expensive bowler and he continues to leak runs. Back of the hand slower
full_text: Tymal Mills to Starc, , Mills has been the most expensive bowler and he continues to leak runs. Back of the hand slower ball, full and in the slot, Starc waits and thumps it over mid-off

short_text: Jordan to Zampa, no run, , another one in the blockhole, Zampa moves leg-side and brings his bat down in time to jam it
full_text: Jordan to Zampa, no run, , another one in the blockhole, Zampa moves leg-side and brings his bat down in time to jam it to backward point. Top-class over from Jordan - just two runs and two wickets. Superb bowling figures too -

short_text: Jordan to Zampa, no run, , yorker outside off, Zampa backs away and connects with thin air. Beaten for pace!
full_text: Jordan to Zampa, no run, , yorker outside off, Zampa backs away and connects with thin air. Beaten for pace!

short_text: Jordan to Starc, 1 run, , in-slanting shortish ball from round the wicket, Starc heaves towards deep square leg. Starc w
full_text: Jordan to Starc, 1 run, , in-slanting shortish ball from round the wicket, Starc heaves towards deep square leg. Starc wants the second but Zampa sends him back

short_text: Jordan to Zampa, 1 run, in the blockhole and outside off, Zampa squeezes it square of the wicket on the off-side
full_text: Jordan to Zampa, 1 run, in the blockhole and outside off, Zampa squeezes it square of the wicket on the off-side

short_text: Jordan to Cummins, Bowled!! , too good for Cummins. The right-hander shuffles and is late to bring his bat down. The bal
full_text: Jordan to Cummins, Bowled!! , too good for Cummins. The right-hander shuffles and is late to bring his bat down. The ball tailed in a bit too before crashing into the base of the off-stump. Cummins's knock - 6, 6, OUT!

short_text: Jordan to Finch, Caught by Bairstow!! , Jordan goes full and straight, Finch doesn't quite middle the lofted off-drive.
full_text: Jordan to Finch, Caught by Bairstow!! , Jordan goes full and straight, Finch doesn't quite middle the lofted off-drive. The ball was dying on him, but Bairstow charged in from long-off and dived forward to complete a superb catch inches above the ground. Bairstow claims that he took it cleanly and Finch walks back.

short_text: Tymal Mills to Cummins, , , six more! Mills shortens his length, it looked like Cummins was expecting this. Clears his l
full_text: Tymal Mills to Cummins, , , six more! Mills shortens his length, it looked like Cummins was expecting this. Clears his left leg and heaves it over deep mid-wicket. Wow! That came right off the meat and it's a biggie. Cummins must be wondering why the top-order batters were struggling on this pitch!

short_text: Tymal Mills to Cummins, , smashed through the line! With mid-off up, Mills bowls a full ball in the slot, Cummins gets f
full_text: Tymal Mills to Cummins, , smashed through the line! With mid-off up, Mills bowls a full ball in the slot, Cummins gets forward, bends his back leg and lofts it over long-off. Easy!

short_text: Tymal Mills to Agar, Caught by Livingstone!! The 47-run stand has been broken! 132kph, shortish ball, Agar made good con
full_text: Tymal Mills to Agar, Caught by Livingstone!! The 47-run stand has been broken! 132kph, shortish ball, Agar made good contact with his pull shot. But he hit it flat and got the placement wrong. Livingstone runs to his left from deep mid-wicket and snaffles with both hands. Made it look so easy. The ball was travelling at a quick pace as Agar had middled that.

short_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, 1 run, 117kph, another slower ball, Finch is early to swing his bat and gets an inside edge onto t
full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, 1 run, 117kph, another slower ball, Finch is early to swing his bat and gets an inside edge onto the pad. The ball dies down on the pitch and the batters steal a single

short_text: Tymal Mills to Agar, 1 run, 110kph, off-speed fullish ball outside off, Agar picks it late and pushes it out to backward
full_text: Tymal Mills to Agar, 1 run, 110kph, off-speed fullish ball outside off, Agar picks it late and pushes it out to backward point for a quick single

short_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, 1 run, very full and outside off, Finch shuffles and clears his front leg to hoick it to long-on's
full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, 1 run, very full and outside off, Finch shuffles and clears his front leg to hoick it to long-on's right

short_text: Woakes to Agar, leg byes, , Big appeal for lbw from Woakes. Nitin Menon turns it down and England have challenged the ca
full_text: Woakes to Agar, leg byes, , Big appeal for lbw from Woakes. Nitin Menon turns it down and England have challenged the call. Fair ball, no bat, but that's 139kph, very full and Agar went deep in his crease and was beaten for pace as he tried to flick. The ball hit his pad and sped away to the fine leg fence

short_text: Woakes to Finch, 1 run, very full ball, driilled towards wide long-on
full_text: Woakes to Finch, 1 run, very full ball, driilled towards wide long-on

short_text: Woakes to Agar, 1 run, 138kph, pitched up ball, flicked to deep mid-wicket
full_text: Woakes to Agar, 1 run, 138kph, pitched up ball, flicked to deep mid-wicket

short_text: Woakes to Agar, , another six. Woakes goes fuller, Agar shuffles and swings his willow to wallop it over deep mid-wicket
full_text: Woakes to Agar, , another six. Woakes goes fuller, Agar shuffles and swings his willow to wallop it over deep mid-wicket fora 83m six!

short_text: Woakes to Agar, , rare mistake from England! Slower ball on a shortish length. Agar went hard on the pull and got a big
full_text: Woakes to Agar, , rare mistake from England! Slower ball on a shortish length. Agar went hard on the pull and got a big top-edge over deep backward square leg. Moeen Ali, the man in the deep, misjudged it. Crossed over the rope and went for the catch only to let it go through his palms. First six of the match! Okay, replays shows Moeen actually stepped on the rope trying to catch, which is why he didn't bother catching that

short_text: Woakes to Agar, , 112kph, slower ball but Woakes errs with his line - down leg
full_text: Woakes to Agar, , 112kph, slower ball but Woakes errs with his line - down leg

full_text: Woakes to Finch, 1 run, 130kph, in-slanting short ball, pulled hard to deep mid-wicket

short_text: Jordan to Finch, 1 run, , Jordan nails the yorker marginally outside off, squeezed out to point and Finch will keep the
full_text: Jordan to Finch, 1 run, , Jordan nails the yorker marginally outside off, squeezed out to point and Finch will keep the strike

short_text: Jordan to Finch, , 136kph, width served, Finch waits for it on the back foot and throws the kitchen sink. Flayed in fron
full_text: Jordan to Finch, , 136kph, width served, Finch waits for it on the back foot and throws the kitchen sink. Flayed in front of backward point and that bulleted away. Not a muscle moved

short_text: Jordan to Agar, 1 run, 137kph, Agar moves leg-side and slaps this short of length ball. That was struck really hard and
full_text: Jordan to Agar, 1 run, 137kph, Agar moves leg-side and slaps this short of length ball. That was struck really hard and Morgan took the pace off it with a dive to his right at extra cover

full_text: Jordan to Finch, 1 run, back of a length, stabbed to sweeper cover off the back foot

full_text: Jordan to Agar, 1 run, 132kph, shortish ball, a miscued pulll to long-on follows

full_text: Jordan to Agar, no run, 135kph, in-slanting short of length ball, Agar moves leg-side and defends. No room on offer

short_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, no run, short and just down leg, Finch lets it go over his left shoulder and is a bit surprised th
full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, no run, short and just down leg, Finch lets it go over his left shoulder and is a bit surprised that it hasn't been deemed wide

full_text: Tymal Mills to Agar, 1 run, in-slanter, leg-lined, nudged to square leg

short_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, 1 run, , Finch is late into his shot. Was a short ball and the right-hander hurriedly pulled. Off
full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, 1 run, , Finch is late into his shot. Was a short ball and the right-hander hurriedly pulled. Off the top-edge and the ball falls wide-and-short of Jordan at mid-on

short_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, no run, 136kph, on a length and outside off, Finch is livid with himself as he misses the late ste
full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, no run, 136kph, on a length and outside off, Finch is livid with himself as he misses the late steer. Beaten on the outside edge

short_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, , , Mills follows it up with a quick one but he's overpitched this wide of off. Finch goes deep an
full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, , , Mills follows it up with a quick one but he's overpitched this wide of off. Finch goes deep and batters it through extra cover. Smashed in the gap and it's back-to-back fours

short_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, , 112kph, slow, full and in the slot. Finch spots it, clears his left leg and thumps it over the b
full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, , 112kph, slow, full and in the slot. Finch spots it, clears his left leg and thumps it over the bowler's head. Didn't bother to run

full_text: Livingstone to Agar, no run, 89kph, full on off, pushed out to backward point and the batsmen rightly decide against the single

full_text: Livingstone to Finch, 1 run, in-drifter, clipped to mid-wicket

full_text: Livingstone to Agar, 1 run, in the air and safe! Flighted offie outside off, Agar slashes for a cut and the ball flies through the vacant point region

short_text: Livingstone to Finch, 1 run, 94kph, shortish leggie outside off, cut past the diving backward point fielder. Moeen mops
full_text: Livingstone to Finch, 1 run, 94kph, shortish leggie outside off, cut past the diving backward point fielder. Moeen mops it up and keeps the batters down to one

full_text: Livingstone to Agar, 1 run, turned into the mid-wicket pocket

full_text: Livingstone to Agar, no run, 95kph, darted in from round the wicket, knocked to the bowler's left and Livingstone dives full-length to make a superb stop

short_text: Adil Rashid to Finch, no run, 87kph, Finch was fortunate to get an inside edge there. Else, that was plumb. Finch doesn'
full_text: Adil Rashid to Finch, no run, 87kph, Finch was fortunate to get an inside edge there. Else, that was plumb. Finch doesn't pick the googly and looks to push from the crease. The ball spun in, trimmed the inner nick before pinning his pads. England appealed but everyone knew there was an inside edge

full_text: Adil Rashid to Agar, 1 run, 84kph, wrong 'un, scythed to deep backward point

full_text: Adil Rashid to Agar, no run, 82kph, leggie, Agar wants to drive against the spin and inside-edges onto his pad

full_text: Adil Rashid to Finch, 1 run, 86kph, in-drifting leggie, Finch gently knocks it to mid-wicket for a single as Rashid gets to the ball

full_text: Livingstone to Agar, no run, fuller and wider, Agar drives off the front foot but straight to cover

short_text: Livingstone to Wade, Caught by Roy!! Holes out at long-on! Tossed up ball, Wade shimmies down the track to play an aeria
full_text: Livingstone to Wade, Caught by Roy!! Holes out at long-on! Tossed up ball, Wade shimmies down the track to play an aerial shot and perishes. The line was outside off and Wade backed himself to clear long-on with his left, but he the timing went awry. Off the lower part of the bat and Roy takes a reverse-cup catch. He was close to the fence but gobbled it up calmly.

short_text: Livingstone to Wade, no run, 89kph, offbreak, on the shorter side and Wade cuts off the back foot. Morgan dives to his r
full_text: Livingstone to Wade, no run, 89kph, offbreak, on the shorter side and Wade cuts off the back foot. Morgan dives to his right at extra cover and makes a fine stop

full_text: Livingstone to Finch, 1 run, 95kph, quick leggie, Finch blunts it into the mid-wicket region for a single

full_text: Livingstone to Wade, 1 run, 92kph, darted in on leg from round the wicket, nurdled to square leg. 50 up for Australia in the 12th over!

full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, no run, 137kph, Mills switches to round the wicket and bowls a full ball, driven firmly but straight to Woakes at mid-off

full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, , slower ball into the pitch, way down leg and Finch lets it be

full_text: Tymal Mills to Wade, 1 run, 139kph, short again, comes in and quicker than Wade expects it to. Wade arches back and awkwardly dangles his bat at it. It was more of ball hitting the bat than the other way around. Splices towards third man for a single

full_text: Tymal Mills to Wade, , 138kph, dug in short with ample width outside off, Wade flashes his bat hard and gets a fat top-edge. Third man runs to his left but it's out of Moeen's reach. Hits the ad-cushions on the half-volley

full_text: Tymal Mills to Finch, 1 run, 138kph, fuller and on the pads, clipped away through mid-wicket and the batters decide against the second

full_text: Livingstone to Finch, no run, oh very full fast, full leggie at 96kph, heading towards off, Finch did well to come forward and present the full face of the bat

full_text: Adil Rashid to Finch, 1 run, goes on the back foot and punches this flatter delivery to long-off

full_text: Adil Rashid to Finch, no run, Buttler goes "Aaahhh" in his appeal for LBW, but most of them knew this pitched outside leg. Finch missed the sweep to this leggie and got hit on the pad

full_text: Adil Rashid to Wade, 1 run, fullish and outside off stump, Wade pushes it towards point

full_text: Adil Rashid to Wade, 2 runs, "yeah, couple, couple," is the shout this time from Wade after rocking back and cutting this short delivery to deep cover

full_text: Adil Rashid to Wade, no run, nice reverse sweep but he picks out Mo at backward point. "Wait on" he shouts after meeting that ball

full_text: Adil Rashid to Finch, 1 run, that's that floaty full ball from Adil Rashid that says, hit me if you can. Finch almost holes out to long-off. On the bounce to the left of the fielder

full_text: Livingstone to Wade, no run, brilliant fielding from Livingstone. Wade advanced and took this on the full, drove to the left of the bowler, full length dive and Livingstone ensured they didn't get a run

full_text: Livingstone to Finch, 2 runs, short and wide, cut away to deep point, Finch not happy he didn't place this behind backward point. Mo patrols the boundary

full_text: Livingstone to Wade, 1 run, bowls the off-spinner to the rightie from round the wicket. Wade leans forward and drives through mid-on

full_text: Livingstone to Finch, 1 run, Livingstone gets turn too. Sliced to the right of short third man, diving stop saves three maybe

full_text: Adil Rashid to Wade, , late cut tried, off the outer half of the bat but Australia don't care. Third man in and this ran away to his right

short_text: Adil Rashid to Finch, 1 run, oh this is a lovely ball! Such a sight to see a spinner bravely float up it to the batter i
full_text: Adil Rashid to Finch, 1 run, oh this is a lovely ball! Such a sight to see a spinner bravely float up it to the batter in this format. Adil Rashid wanted Finch to go hard for the cover drive looking to get an edge, Finch played it calmly to deep cover

full_text: Adil Rashid to Wade, 1 run, fullish and angling away, Wade drives to long-off

full_text: Adil Rashid to Wade, no run, flighted delivery just outside off stump, Wade defends

short_text: Adil Rashid to Stoinis, Lbw!! Brilliant googly, hits the pads, England went "Yeaaahhh" in their appeal and Nitin Menon's
full_text: Adil Rashid to Stoinis, Lbw!! Brilliant googly, hits the pads, England went "Yeaaahhh" in their appeal and Nitin Menon's finger went up before you could blink. And Stoinis doesn't bother Finch for the review. Fast, gripping googly from Adil Rashid. Stoinis was on the back foot, this one rushed him, bat didn't down in time and that's absolutely plumb in front, smashing the leg stump at least. Morgan wants to kill this game with his changes.

full_text: Woakes to Finch, no run, tries to run at the bowler but Woakes drops it short and keeps Finch calm, defended to mid-wicket

full_text: Jordan to Finch, 2 runs, short of length delivery nipping back in, Finch plays a rushed flick off the inner half of the bat, rolls through square leg

full_text: Jordan to Finch, no run, now the Aussie captain down bring the bat down very straight, which is why he was able to cover for the late inswing and meet this length ball with the full face of the bat

short_text: Jordan to Finch, no run, good review that from England. Pretty bold, tight call from umpire Nitin Menon. Some bug seems
full_text: Jordan to Finch, no run, good review that from England. Pretty bold, tight call from umpire Nitin Menon. Some bug seems to have crept into that Aussie dressing room. Their batters are playing out of really poor positions. Yet again the feet towards extra-cover and then the target targets mid-wicket. How on earth in cricket can you meet the ball? Jordan got this length delivery to nip back in, impact on leg, just clipping top of leg

short_text: Woakes to Stoinis, no run, outside off stump, left alone
full_text: Woakes to Stoinis, no run, outside off stump, left alone

short_text: Woakes to Maxwell, Lbw!! What are you doing Australia? Warner, Smith, Maxwell all out to schoolboy errors. Maxwell never
full_text: Woakes to Maxwell, Lbw!! What are you doing Australia? Warner, Smith, Maxwell all out to schoolboy errors. Maxwell never looked comfortable against the swing and seam, just blindly swung across the line to his nip-backer and Woakes knew he had his man as soon as the ball pinged the pad. Maxwell's front foot pointing towards extra-cover and then he tries a slog to the leg-side. What a horrible tangle he got into. Discussed with Finch for the review. They probably thought they would save the review with umpire's call but ball tracker said smashing leg stump. England did nothing special thus far. Just kept the ball in the same area and let the batters do the mistake. Morgan and captaincy, the love story continues.

short_text: Woakes to Maxwell, 2 runs, this is ball hitting the bat yet again from good length. The bat turns in Maxwell's hands and
full_text: Woakes to Maxwell, 2 runs, this is ball hitting the bat yet again from good length. The bat turns in Maxwell's hands and rolls through mid-wicket

short_text: Woakes to Maxwell, no run, Maxwell not middling his pushes yet. Seems like he wants to go over the top, but checks the d
full_text: Woakes to Maxwell, no run, Maxwell not middling his pushes yet. Seems like he wants to go over the top, but checks the drive because it's not very full or very short, played to mid-on

short_text: Woakes to Maxwell, no run, simple length delivery just around off stump, Maxwell defends
full_text: Woakes to Maxwell, no run, simple length delivery just around off stump, Maxwell defends

short_text: Woakes to Maxwell, 2 runs, fullish and on the stumps, Maxwell plays off the inner half of the bat and it rolls to deep m
full_text: Woakes to Maxwell, 2 runs, fullish and on the stumps, Maxwell plays off the inner half of the bat and it rolls to deep mid-wicket

full_text: Jordan to Maxwell, 1 run, on the stumps, wristed towards square leg

full_text: Jordan to Maxwell, no run, blocked covering the line

full_text: Jordan to Finch, 1 run, inside edge onto pad as Finch looks to work this, they get a run

full_text: Jordan to Maxwell, 1 run, worked away to mid-on

short_text: Jordan to Maxwell, no run, . Swinging away late from good length and Maxwell plays with his feet stuck on the crease. Co
full_text: Jordan to Maxwell, no run, . Swinging away late from good length and Maxwell plays with his feet stuck on the crease. Comprehensively beaten on the outside edge

short_text: Jordan to Smith, Caught by Woakes!! Ohhh stunning grab by Woakes at mid-on. So this was a total miscue from Smith. Mid-o
full_text: Jordan to Smith, Caught by Woakes!! Ohhh stunning grab by Woakes at mid-on. So this was a total miscue from Smith. Mid-on wasn't on the edge. Woakes was in two minds, whether to turn back and get the ball or just backtrack with a few steps. He realises the ball is going to clear him so quickly has the presence of mind to stretch his right hand full length with a bit of dive totally out of position. But those bucket hands get it. What a start this is for England. Horrible, horrible shot from Smith. This was a harmless short ball way outside off stump, tell your coach you are trying to drag it to leg and he will give you a tight slap. The ball is swinging away too. Caught the toe-end of the bat and flew to mid-on. Should have surely gone through the off-side!

short_text: Woakes to Smith, 1 run, off the inside edge through backward square leg for a run
full_text: Woakes to Smith, 1 run, off the inside edge through backward square leg for a run

short_text: Woakes to Smith, no run, appeal for LBW, not given. Review? No. Woakes looks trouble Smith around his pads with his thin
full_text: Woakes to Smith, no run, appeal for LBW, not given. Review? No. Woakes looks trouble Smith around his pads with his thing of moving far across the stumps. Smith missed the flick, impact in line with leg but was just missing the sticks

short_text: Woakes to Smith, no run, now Smith shoulders arms. Yes, the bowler demanded that sort of respect
full_text: Woakes to Smith, no run, now Smith shoulders arms. Yes, the bowler demanded that sort of respect

short_text: Woakes to Smith, no run, Steve Smith beaten by seam movement. I thought the Ashes was still a couple of months away! Pro
full_text: Woakes to Smith, no run, Steve Smith beaten by seam movement. I thought the Ashes was still a couple of months away! Proper Test match line outside off stump, back of a length, pushing the batter back, Smith fully opened up by the away movement, lucky not to have nicked that off

short_text: Woakes to Warner, Caught by Buttler!! Davey Warner nicking off outside off against England. Oh, this story continues. He
full_text: Woakes to Warner, Caught by Buttler!! Davey Warner nicking off outside off against England. Oh, this story continues. He is livid with himself, literally rips the hand off the glove as he walks back. Simple back of a length delivery outside off stump, Warner unsure of his defence, not fully back, not forward, just an ill-advised poke away from the body looking to a late guide behind point. Thin edge through to Buttler, who didn't even celebrate much. Kind of "yeah I knew he will nick off" gentle throw of the ball from Buttler.

full_text: Woakes to Finch, 1 run, bit of curve back into the right-hander on this good length delivery, Finch knocks it to mid-on

full_text: Agar to Bairstow, 1 run, driven away to deep point and Australia have been absolutely bashed, bruised and buried by resounding authority from their arch-rivals. I0$

full_text: Agar to Buttler, 1 run, to short third man, looks to scoop and decides to dab it away at the last moment. A low full toss from Agar and it has been placed to the left of Finch at short third

full_text: Agar to Bairstow, byes, , they are never content are this England side. Not in T20 cricket. Bairstow throws the kitchen sink outside off and it just skids away past the outside edge beating the diving effort of Finch to his right

full_text: Zampa to Bairstow, 1 run, looks to slog and drags the full delivery wide of deep mid-wicket

full_text: Zampa to Bairstow, , a wink to Bairstow's former opening partner. That's how casual it is. They are marmalizing the Aussies.... Down on one knee, picks the fullish delivery from middle and leg and the next thing Zampa sees is the ball landing deep in the mid-wicket stands...

short_text: Zampa to Bairstow, , ah Jonny Bairstow! What bottom hand power. Gee, the England skipper is befuddled seeing the power t
full_text: Zampa to Bairstow, , ah Jonny Bairstow! What bottom hand power. Gee, the England skipper is befuddled seeing the power that YJB generated despite sitting on the back foot. Just stayed still and golfswung Zampa into the long-on orbit...

full_text: Zampa to Buttler, 1 run, uses his feet and whips the full delivery off his pads wide of long-on

full_text: Zampa to Buttler, , Jos Buttler is in no mood to sit back. He is hammering them all corners. Jumps out of his crease, gets closer to the pitch of the delivery and thrashes Zampa deep into the long-on stands

full_text: Agar to Bairstow, no run, full and fired in at the pads, Bairstow tries to help it away with a slog but fails to put bat to ball. Hits the toe and deflects into the leg-side

short_text: Agar to Malan, Caught by Wade!! Flattish delivery, Malan pushes at it and gets a faint edge for the keeper to take it. T
full_text: Agar to Malan, Caught by Wade!! Flattish delivery, Malan pushes at it and gets a faint edge for the keeper to take it. That was a quicker one outside off, Malan pushed at it and got a feather. Good take indeed from Wade. A wicket against the run of play. Some time for Australia as well to catch their breathe.

full_text: Zampa to Buttler, , and the reverse smack. Four more. MOCKERY! Yes, this is some sort of a warning bell that England have issued to every team. They just aren't stopping

full_text: Zampa to Buttler, , the BUTTLER has dished out another sixer and brings about a fifty. He chipped out and slammed it over long-on for a six which scaled 102 metres. Superb hitting

full_text: Starc to Buttler, , the midas pockets another four. Again a little backing away movement to put the bowler off. Starc dropped it short, Buttler got hold of it and slapped the pull wide of deep mid for a four.

full_text: Starc to Buttler, , Buttler is putting on a show on a tough wicket for batters. He isn't allowing Starc to bowl his lines. Backed away a little, then was still and creamed the full toss down the ground for a four. Mid-off was in

full_text: Starc to Malan, 1 run, fine looking shot to fine leg for a single as the ball angled in

full_text: Starc to Malan, no run, and Starc gets it on the money with an inch perfect yorker! Malan somehow gets the bat down and keeps it out. He is going for it as he knows Australia need a barrage of wickets to come back into this

full_text: Zampa to Roy, Lbw!! One shot too many and Roy is gone. Wanted to dominate Zampa who has had a good series so far. That was a flatter ball and Roy went for the reverse hack and missed the line. The ball hit him in front of the sticks and Australia desperate for a wicket went for the review. A little edge here and there could have saved him but wasn't to be.

full_text: Starc to Roy, no run, some nice shape bending into the batter and has been pushed back to the bowler. Nice curve into the batter
